summary: Create gui/src/components/ directory for shared Splitter component; Write failing tests for Splitter component: renders a draggable splitter bar with data-testid, applies correct orientation (horizontal/vertical), mouse drag updates callback with delta position, respects min/max size constraints, has appropriate cursor styling.; Implement Splitter component (gui/src/components/Splitter.tsx + Splitter.module.css): a thin draggable bar that fires onResize(delta) on mousemove during drag. Props: orientation ('horizontal'|'vertical'), onResize callback, data-testid. Handles mousedown/mousemove/mouseup lifecycle, sets cursor on document during drag.; Write failing tests for wired App layout — renders all real components: test that App mounts and contains viewport-canvas (from Viewport), editor-container (from Editor), property-editor (from PropertyEditor), constraint-panel (from ConstraintPanel), toolbar (from Toolbar), status-bar (from StatusBar). Must mock bridge (getInitialState, event listeners) and Three.js/Viewport internals. Verify toolbar is at top and status bar at bottom via DOM order.; Rewrite App.tsx to compose all real components into the layout: create engineStore, editorStore, selectionStore. Render Toolbar at top, 3-column main area (Editor left, Viewport center, PropertyEditor+ConstraintPanel right), StatusBar at bottom. Pass store data as props to each component. Wire setParameter callback through bridge.setParameter. Wire toolbar onExport/onFitToView stubs.; Write failing tests for resizable splitters in App: verify two Splitter components exist between left-center and center-right columns. Test that dragging a splitter updates panel widths (via CSS custom properties or inline styles).; Add resizable panel layout to App: integrate Splitter components between editor/viewport and viewport/side-panel. Track panel widths with createSignal, update on Splitter onResize. Apply widths via grid-template-columns with CSS custom properties. Update App.module.css for the full grid layout with toolbar row, main resizable area, and status bar row.; Write failing test for initial state loading on mount: mock bridge.getInitialState to return test GuiState (meshes, values, constraints, files). Verify that after mount, engineStore is populated (values appear in PropertyEditor), editorStore has files open, and subscribeToEvents was called.

Fix test parse errors (JSX in vi.mock factories), add beforeEach mock reset, implement reactive document.title (createEffect watching activeFile + evalStatus), add horizontal Splitter in side panel between PropertyEditor and ConstraintPanel with draggable resize. All 25 tests pass (7 Splitter + 18 App). All 12 plan steps now complete.

Complete task 98: Tauri bundler + CLI integration + distribution. Generated placeholder icon files (PNG/ICO/ICNS) for Tauri v2 bundler. Added 'gui <file>' subcommand to reify-cli that validates file existence and .ri extension, then spawns the reify-gui binary. Configured tauri.conf.json with bundle.active=true, cross-platform targets (deb/appimage/dmg/msi/nsis), icon paths, .ri file associations, and OCCT shared library resources. Added OCCT bundling documentation. All 15 CLI tests pass (3 smoke + 6 gui + 2 lsp + 2 lsp_protocol + 2 build).
